no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the symptoms (race condition in cancelReadCloser during construction with context cancellation) and behavioral requirements but does not specify the exact fix (changing crc.Close() to crc.close() in AfterFunc). A developer must reason about the implementation to determine what to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The test `test_close_method_calls_once` asserts closeCount.Load() == 1 after cancel() + 3 manual Close() calls. The gold solution changes `crc.Close()` to `crc.close()` in the AfterFunc callback, relying on the existing `sync.Once` in `close` to deduplicate. An alternative correct fix might use a mutex or separate sync mechanism that still calls the underlying Close() but guards it differently - such a fix could result in closeCount being >1 if it wraps Close() differently. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All tests execute Go code via subprocess (go test, go build, go vet, gofmt), compile and run actual test functions, and check return codes and race detector output. No test merely greps source files for text patterns.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests (TestEncodePlatforms, TestOptionWithAPIVersion, go vet, gofmt, go build) test real existing functionality. A stub implementation would fail these since they exercise actual client package code. The race condition tests would detect actual data races via the race detector, which cannot be satisfied by a no-op.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 6 behavioral requirements from instruction.md are tested: (1) no data race - test_race_condition_fix, (2) close after cancel - test_cancelreadcloser_close_after_context_cancel, (3) close once - test_close_method_calls_once, (4) existing tests pass - test_repo_utils_encode_platforms and test_repo_client_options, (5) go vet and gofmt - test_repo_go_vet and test_repo_gofmt, (6) build succeeds - test_repo_client_build.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_race_condition_fix, test_close_method_calls_once, etc.), have docstrings explaining purpose, and are logically grouped by concern (race fix, close behavior, compilation, existing tests).
